info.name: 1 Corinthians
info.desc: Paul's First Letter to the Corinthians

1: From Paul, who was called by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from our brother Sosthenes —
2: To the church of God which is in Corinth, to all who are called to be God's holy people, who belong to him in union with Christ Jesus, together with all people everywhere who worship our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours:
Acts 18.1
3: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
Blessings in Christ
4: I always give thanks to my God for you because of the grace he has given you through Christ Jesus.
Blessings in Christ
5: For in union with Christ you have become rich in all things, including all speech and all knowledge.
Blessings in Christ
6: The message about Christ has become so firmly established in you
Blessings in Christ
7: that you have not failed to receive a single blessing, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
Blessings in Christ
8: He will also keep you firm to the end, so that you will be faultless on the Day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Blessings in Christ
9: God is to be trusted, the God who called you to have fellowship with his Son Jesus Christ, our Lord.
Divisions in the Church
10: By the authority of our Lord Jesus Christ I appeal to all of you, my brothers and sisters, to agree in what you say, so that there will be no divisions among you. Be completely united, with only one thought and one purpose.
Divisions in the Church
11: For some people from Chloe's family have told me quite plainly, my friends, that there are quarrels among you.
Divisions in the Church
12: Let me put it this way: each one of you says something different. One says, “I follow Paul”; another, “I follow Apollos”; another, “I follow Peter”; and another, “I follow Christ”.
Divisions in the Church
Acts 18.24
13:
Christ has been divided
 into groups! Was it Paul who died on the cross for you? Were you baptized as Paul's disciples?
Divisions in the Church
1.13 Christ has been divided; some manuscripts have Christ cannot be divided.
14: I thank God that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
Divisions in the Church
15: No one can say, then, that you were baptized as my disciples.
Divisions in the Church
16: (Oh yes, I also baptized Stephanas and his family; but I can't remember whether I baptized anyone else.)
Divisions in the Church
17: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the Good News, and to tell it without using the language of human wisdom, in order to make sure that Christ's death on the cross is not robbed of its power.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
18: For the message about Christ's death on the cross is nonsense to those who are being lost; but for us who are being saved it is God's power.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
19:
The scripture says:
“I will destroy the wisdom of the wise
and set aside the understanding of the scholars.”
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
20: So then, where does that leave the wise? or the scholars? or the skilful debaters of this world? God has shown that this world's wisdom is foolishness!
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
21: For God in his wisdom made it impossible for people to know him by means of their own wisdom. Instead, by means of the so-called “foolish” message we preach, God decided to save those who believe.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
22: Jews want miracles for proof, and Greeks look for wisdom.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
23: As for us, we proclaim the crucified Christ, a message that is offensive to the Jews and nonsense to the Gentiles;
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
24: but for those whom God has called, both Jews and Gentiles, this message is Christ, who is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
25: For what seems to be God's foolishness is wiser than human wisdom, and what seems to be God's weakness is stronger than human strength.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
26: Now remember what you were, my brothers and sisters, when God called you. From the human point of view few of you were wise or powerful or of high social standing.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
27: God purposely chose what the world considers nonsense in order to shame the wise, and he chose what the world considers weak in order to shame the powerful.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
28: He chose what the world looks down on and despises, and thinks is nothing, in order to destroy what the world thinks is important.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
29: This means that no one can boast in God's presence.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
30: But God has brought you into union with Christ Jesus, and God has made Christ to be our wisdom. By him we are put right with God; we become God's holy people and are set free.
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God
31: So then, as the scripture says, “Whoever wants to boast must boast of what the Lord has done.”
Christ the Power and the Wisdom of God

1:
When I came to you, my brothers and sisters, to preach God's secret truth,
 I did not use big words and great learning.
The Message about the Crucified Christ
2.1 God's secret truth; some manuscripts have the testimony about God.
2: For while I was with you, I made up my mind to forget everything except Jesus Christ and especially his death on the cross.
The Message about the Crucified Christ
3: So when I came to you, I was weak and trembled all over with fear,
The Message about the Crucified Christ
Acts 18.9
4: and my teaching and message were not delivered with skilful words of human wisdom, but with convincing proof of the power of God's Spirit.
The Message about the Crucified Christ
5: Your faith, then, does not rest on human wisdom but on God's power.
God's Wisdom
6: Yet I do proclaim a message of wisdom to those who are spiritually mature. But it is not the wisdom that belongs to this world or to the powers that rule this world — powers that are losing their power.
God's Wisdom
7: The wisdom I proclaim is God's hidden wisdom, which he had already chosen for our glory even before the world was made.
God's Wisdom
8: None of the rulers of this world knew this wisdom. If they had known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.
God's Wisdom
9:
However, as the scripture says:
“What no one ever saw or heard,
what no one ever thought could happen,
is the very thing God prepared for those who love him.”
God's Wisdom
10:
But
 it was to us that God made known his secret by means of his Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, even the hidden depths of God's purposes.
God's Wisdom
2.10 But; some manuscripts have For.
11: It is only the spirit within people that knows all about them; in the same way, only God's Spirit knows all about God.
God's Wisdom
12: We have not received this world's spirit; instead, we have received the Spirit sent by God, so that we may know all that God has given us.
God's Wisdom
13: So then, we do not speak in words taught by human wisdom, but in words taught by the Spirit, as we explain spiritual truths to those who have the Spirit.
God's Wisdom
2.13 to those who have the Spirit; or with words given by the Spirit.
14: Whoever does not have the Spirit cannot receive the gifts that come from God's Spirit. Such people really do not understand them; they are nonsense to them, because their value can be judged only on a spiritual basis.
God's Wisdom
15: Whoever has the Spirit, however, is able to judge the value of everything, but no one is able to judge him.
God's Wisdom
16:
As the scripture says:
“Who knows the mind of the Lord?
Who is able to give him advice?”
We, however, have the mind of Christ.
God's Wisdom

1: As a matter of fact, my brothers and sisters, I could not talk to you as I talk to people who have the Spirit; I had to talk to you as though you belonged to this world, as children in the Christian faith.
Servants of God
2: I had to feed you with milk, not solid food, because you were not ready for it. And even now you are not ready for it,
Servants of God
3: because you still live as the people of this world live. When there is jealousy among you and you quarrel with one another, doesn't this prove that you belong to this world, living by its standards?
Servants of God
4: When one of you says, “I follow Paul”, and another, “I follow Apollos” — aren't you acting like worldly people?
Servants of God
5: After all, who is Apollos? And who is Paul? We are simply God's servants, by whom you were led to believe. Each one of us does the work which the Lord gave him to do:
Servants of God
6: I sowed the seed, Apollos watered the plant, but it was God who made the plant grow.
Servants of God
7: The one who sows and the one who waters really do not matter. It is God who matters, because he makes the plant grow.
Servants of God
8: There is no difference between the one who sows and the one who waters; God will reward each one according to the work each has done.
Servants of God
9:
For we are partners working together for God, and you are God's field.
You are also God's building.
Servants of God
10: Using the gift that God gave me, I did the work of an expert builder and laid the foundation, and someone else is building on it. But each one must be careful how he builds.
Servants of God
11: For God has already placed Jesus Christ as the one and only foundation, and no other foundation can be laid.
Servants of God
12: Some will use gold or silver or precious stones in building on the foundation; others will use wood or grass or straw.
Servants of God
13: And the quality of each person's work will be seen when the Day of Christ exposes it. For on that Day fire will reveal everyone's work; the fire will test it and show its real quality.
Servants of God
14: If what was built on the foundation survives the fire, the builder will receive a reward.
Servants of God
15: But if anyone's work is burnt up, then he will lose it; but he himself will be saved, as if he had escaped through the fire.
Servants of God
16: Surely you know that you are God's temple and that God's Spirit lives in you!
Servants of God
17: So if anyone destroys God's temple, God will destroy him. For God's temple is holy, and you yourselves are his temple.
Servants of God
18: No one should fool himself. If anyone among you thinks that he is wise by this world's standards, he should become a fool, in order to be really wise.
Servants of God
19: For what this world considers to be wisdom is nonsense in God's sight. As the scripture says, “God traps the wise in their cleverness”;
Servants of God
Job 5.13
20: and another scripture says, “The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are worthless.”
Servants of God
21: No one, then, should boast about what human beings can do. Actually everything belongs to you:
Servants of God
22: Paul, Apollos, and Peter; this world, life and death, the present and the future — all these are yours,
Servants of God
23: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
Servants of God

1: You should think of us as Christ's servants, who have been put in charge of God's secret truths.
Apostles of Christ
2: The one thing required of such servants is that they be faithful to their master.
Apostles of Christ
3: Now, I am not at all concerned about being judged by you or by any human standard; I don't even pass judgement on myself.
Apostles of Christ
4: My conscience is clear, but that does not prove that I am really innocent. The Lord is the one who passes judgement on me.
Apostles of Christ
5: So you should not pass judgement on anyone before the right time comes. Final judgement must wait until the Lord comes; he will bring to light the dark secrets and expose the hidden purposes of people's minds. And then all will receive from God the praise they deserve.
Apostles of Christ
6: For your sake, my brothers and sisters, I have applied all this to Apollos and me, using the two of us as an example, so that you may learn what the saying means, “Observe the proper rules.” None of you should be proud of one person and despise another.
Apostles of Christ
7: Who made you superior to others? Didn't God give you everything you have? Well, then, how can you boast, as if what you have were not a gift?
Apostles of Christ
8: Do you already have everything you need? Are you already rich? Have you become kings, even though we are not? Well, I wish you really were kings, so that we could be kings together with you.
Apostles of Christ
9: For it seems to me that God has given the very last place to us apostles, like people condemned to die in public as a spectacle for the whole world of angels and of humanity.
Apostles of Christ
10: For Christ's sake we are fools; but you are wise in union with Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! We are despised, but you are honoured!
Apostles of Christ
11: To this very moment we go hungry and thirsty; we are clothed in rags; we are beaten; we wander from place to place;
Apostles of Christ
12: we wear ourselves out with hard work. When we are cursed, we bless; when we are persecuted, we endure;
Apostles of Christ
Acts 18.3
13: when we are insulted, we answer with kind words. We are no more than this world's refuse; we are the scum of the earth to this very moment!
Apostles of Christ
14: I write this to you, not because I want to make you feel ashamed, but to instruct you as my own dear children.
Apostles of Christ
15: For even if you have 10,000 guardians in your Christian life, you have only one father. For in your life in union with Christ Jesus I have become your father by bringing the Good News to you.
Apostles of Christ
16: I beg you, then, to follow my example.
Apostles of Christ
1 Cor 11.1; Phil 3.17
17: For this purpose I am sending to you Timothy, who is my own dear and faithful son in the Christian life. He will remind you of the principles which I follow in the new life in union with Christ Jesus and which I teach in all the churches everywhere.
Apostles of Christ
18: Some of you have become proud because you have thought that I would not be coming to visit you.
Apostles of Christ
19: If the Lord is willing, however, I will come to you soon, and then I will find out for myself the power which these proud people have, and not just what they say.
Apostles of Christ
20: For the Kingdom of God is not a matter of words but of power.
Apostles of Christ
21: Which do you prefer? Shall I come to you with a whip, or in a spirit of love and gentleness?
Apostles of Christ

1: Now, it is actually being said that there is sexual immorality among you so terrible that not even the heathen would be guilty of it. I am told that a man is sleeping with his stepmother!
Immorality in the Church
Deut 22.30
2: How, then, can you be proud? On the contrary, you should be filled with sadness, and the man who has done such a thing should be expelled from your fellowship.
Immorality in the Church
3-4: And even though I am far away from you in body, still I am there with you in spirit; and as though I were there with you, I have in the name of our Lord Jesus already passed judgement on the man who has done this terrible thing. As you meet together, and I meet with you in my spirit, by the power of our Lord Jesus present with us,
5: you are to hand this man over to Satan for his body to be destroyed, so that his spirit may be saved in the Day of the Lord.
Immorality in the Church
6: It is not right for you to be proud! You know the saying, “A little bit of yeast makes the whole batch of dough rise.”
Immorality in the Church
Gal 5.9
7: You must remove the old yeast of sin so that you will be entirely pure. Then you will be like a new batch of dough without any yeast, as indeed I know you actually are. For our Passover Festival is ready, now that Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.
Immorality in the Church
8: Let us celebrate our Passover, then, not with bread having the old yeast of sin and wickedness, but with the bread that has no yeast, the bread of purity and truth.
Immorality in the Church
Ex 13.7; Deut 16.3
9: In the letter that I wrote you I told you not to associate with immoral people.
Immorality in the Church
10: Now I did not mean pagans who are immoral or greedy or are thieves or who worship idols. To avoid them you would have to get out of the world completely.
Immorality in the Church
11: What I meant was that you should not associate with a person who calls himself a believer but is immoral or greedy or worships idols or is a slanderer or a drunkard or a thief. Don't even sit down to eat with such a person.
Immorality in the Church
12-13: After all, it is none of my business to judge outsiders. God will judge them. But should you not judge the members of your own fellowship? As the scripture says, “Remove the evil person from your group.”

1: If any of you have a dispute with another Christian, how dare you go before heathen judges instead of letting God's people settle the matter?
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
2: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? Well, then, if you are to judge the world, aren't you capable of judging small matters?
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
3: Do you not know that we shall judge the angels? How much more, then, the things of this life!
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
4: If such matters come up, are you going to take them to be settled by people who have no standing in the church?
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
5: Shame on you! Surely there is at least one wise person in your fellowship who can settle a dispute between fellow-Christians.
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
6: Instead, one Christian goes to court against another and lets unbelievers judge the case!
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
7: The very fact that you have legal disputes among yourselves shows that you have failed completely. Would it not be better for you to be wronged? Would it not be better for you to be robbed?
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
8: Instead, you yourselves wrong one another and rob one another, even your own brothers and sisters!
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
9: Surely you know that the wicked will not possess God's Kingdom. Do not fool yourselves; people who are immoral or who worship idols or are adulterers or homosexual perverts
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
10: or who steal or are greedy or are drunkards or who slander others or are thieves — none of these will possess God's Kingdom.
Lawsuits against Fellow-Christians
11: Some of you were like that. But you have been purified from sin; you have been dedicated to God; you have been put right with God by the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
12: Someone will say, “I am allowed to do anything.” Yes; but not everything is good for you. I could say that I am allowed to do anything, but I am not going to let anything make me its slave.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
13: Someone else will say, “Food is for the stomach, and the stomach is for food.” Yes; but God will put an end to both. The body is not to be used for sexual immorality, but to serve the Lord; and the Lord provides for the body.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
14: God raised the Lord from death, and he will also raise us by his power.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
15: You know that your bodies are parts of the body of Christ. Shall I take a part of Christ's body and make it part of the body of a prostitute? Impossible!
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
16: Or perhaps you don't know that the man who joins his body to a prostitute becomes physically one with her? The scripture says quite plainly, “The two will become one body.”
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
Gen 2.24
17: But he who joins himself to the Lord becomes spiritually one with him.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
18: Avoid immorality. Any other sin a man commits does not affect his body; but the man who is guilty of sexual immorality sins against his own body.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
19: Don't you know that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit, who lives in you and who was given to you by God? You do not belong to yourselves but to God;
Use your Bodies for God's Glory
20: he bought you for a price. So use your bodies for God's glory.
Use your Bodies for God's Glory

1:
Now, to deal with the matters you wrote about.
A man does well not to marry.
Questions about Marriage
; 7.1 A man does well not to marry; or You say that a man does well not to marry.
2: But because there is so much immorality, every man should have his own wife, and every woman should have her own husband.
Questions about Marriage
3: A man should fulfil his duty as a husband, and a woman should fulfil her duty as a wife, and each should satisfy the other's needs.
Questions about Marriage
4: A wife is not the master of her own body, but her husband is; in the same way a husband is not the master of his own body, but his wife is.
Questions about Marriage
5: Do not deny yourselves to each other, unless you first agree to do so for a while in order to spend your time in prayer; but then resume normal marital relations. In this way you will be kept from giving in to Satan's temptation because of your lack of self-control.
Questions about Marriage
6: I tell you this not as an order, but simply as a concession.
Questions about Marriage
7: Actually I would prefer that all of you were as I am; but each one has a special gift from God, one person this gift, another one that gift.
Questions about Marriage
8: Now, to the unmarried and to the widows I say that it would be better for you to continue to live alone as I do.
Questions about Marriage
9: But if you cannot restrain your desires, go ahead and marry — it is better to marry than to burn with passion.
Questions about Marriage
10: For married people I have a command which is not my own but the Lord's: a wife must not leave her husband;
Questions about Marriage
11: but if she does, she must remain single or else be reconciled to her husband; and a husband must not divorce his wife.
Questions about Marriage
12: To the others I say (I, myself, not the Lord): if a Christian man has a wife who is an unbeliever and she agrees to go on living with him, he must not divorce her.
Questions about Marriage
13: And if a Christian woman is married to a man who is an unbeliever and he agrees to go on living with her, she must not divorce him.
Questions about Marriage
14: For the unbelieving husband is made acceptable to God by being united to his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made acceptable to God by being united to her Christian husband. If this were not so, their children would be like pagan children; but as it is, they are acceptable to God.
Questions about Marriage
15: However, if the one who is not a believer wishes to leave the Christian partner, let it be so. In such cases the Christian partner, whether husband or wife, is free to act. God has called you to live in peace.
Questions about Marriage
16:
How can you be sure, Christian wife, that you will not save
 your husband? Or how can you be sure, Christian husband, that you will not save your wife?
Live as God Called You
7.16 How can you be sure… that you will not save; or How do you know… that you will save.
17: Each of you should go on living according to the Lord's gift to you, and as you were when God called you. This is the rule I teach in all the churches.
Live as God Called You
18: If a circumcised man has accepted God's call, he should not try to remove the marks of circumcision; if an uncircumcised man has accepted God's call, he should not get circumcised.
Live as God Called You
19: For whether or not a man is circumcised means nothing; what matters is to obey God's commandments.
Live as God Called You
20: Each of you should remain as you were when you accepted God's call.
Live as God Called You
21: Were you a slave when God called you? Well, never mind; but if you have a chance to become free, use it.
Live as God Called You
7.21 but if you have a chance to become free, use it; or but even if you have a chance to become free, choose rather to make the best of your condition as a slave.
22: For a slave who has been called by the Lord is free for the Lord; in the same way a free person who has been called by Christ is his slave.
Live as God Called You
23: God bought you for a price; so do not become slaves of human beings.
Live as God Called You
24: My friends, each of you should remain in fellowship with God in the same condition as you were when you were called.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
25: Now, concerning what you wrote about unmarried people: I do not have a command from the Lord, but I give my opinion as one who by the Lord's mercy is worthy of trust.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
26: Considering the present distress, I think it is better for a man to stay as he is.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
27: Have you got a wife? Then don't try to get rid of her. Are you unmarried? Then don't look for a wife.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
28: But if you do marry, you haven't committed a sin; and if an unmarried woman marries, she hasn't committed a sin. But I would rather spare you the everyday troubles that married people will have.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
29: What I mean, my friends, is this: there is not much time left, and from now on married men should live as though they were not married;
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
30: those who weep, as though they were not sad; those who laugh, as though they were not happy; those who buy, as though they did not own what they bought;
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
31: those who deal in material goods, as though they were not fully occupied with them. For this world, as it is now, will not last much longer.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
32: I would like you to be free from worry. An unmarried man concerns himself with the Lord's work, because he is trying to please the Lord.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
33: But a married man concerns himself with worldly matters, because he wants to please his wife;
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
34: and so he is pulled in two directions. An unmarried woman or a virgin concerns herself with the Lord's work, because she wants to be dedicated both in body and spirit; but a married woman concerns herself with worldly matters, because she wants to please her husband.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
35: I am saying this because I want to help you. I am not trying to put restrictions on you. Instead, I want you to do what is right and proper, and to give yourselves completely to the Lord's service without any reservation.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
36:
In the case of an engaged couple who have decided not to marry: if the man feels that he is not acting properly towards the young woman and if his passions are too strong and he feels that they ought to marry, then they should get married, as he wants to.
 There is no sin in this.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows

7.36 an engaged couple… as he wants to; or a man and his unmarried daughter: if he feels that he is not acting properly towards her, and if she is at the right age to marry, then he should do as he wishes and let her get married.
37:
But if a man, without being forced to do so, has firmly made up his mind not to marry,
 and if he has his will under complete control and has already decided in his own mind what to do — then he does well not to marry the young woman.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
7.37 not to marry; or not to let his daughter get married.; 7.37 marry the young woman; or let her get married.
38:
So the man who marries
 does well, but the one who doesn't marry
 does even better.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
7.38 doesn't marry; or doesn't let her get married.; 7.38 marries; or lets his daughter get married.
39: A married woman is not free as long as her husband lives; but if her husband dies, then she is free to be married to any man she wishes, but only if he is a Christian.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows
40: She will be happier, however, if she stays as she is. That is my opinion, and I think that I too have God's Spirit.
Questions about the Unmarried and the Widows

1:
Now, concerning what you wrote about food offered to idols.
It is true, of course, that “all of us have knowledge”, as they say. Such knowledge, however, puffs a person up with pride; but love builds up.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
2: Those who think they know something really don't know as they ought to know.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
3: But the person who loves God is known by him.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
4: So then, about eating the food offered to idols: we know that an idol stands for something that does not really exist; we know that there is only the one God.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
5: Even if there are so-called “gods”, whether in heaven or on earth, and even though there are many of these “gods” and “lords”,
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
6: yet there is for us only one God, the Father, who is the Creator of all things and for whom we live; and there is only one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom all things were created and through whom we live.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
7: But not everyone knows this truth. Some people have been so used to idols that to this day when they eat such food they still think of it as food that belongs to an idol; their conscience is weak, and they feel they are defiled by the food.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
8: Food, however, will not improve our relations with God; we shall not lose anything if we do not eat, nor shall we gain anything if we do eat.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
9: Be careful, however, not to let your freedom of action make those who are weak in the faith fall into sin.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
10: Suppose a person whose conscience is weak in this matter sees you, who have so-called “knowledge”, eating in the temple of an idol; will not this encourage him to eat food offered to idols?
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
11: And so this weak person, your brother for whom Christ died, will perish because of your “knowledge”!
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
12: And in this way you will be sinning against Christ by sinning against your Christian brothers and sisters and wounding their weak conscience.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols
13: So then, if food makes my brother or sister sin, I will never eat meat again, so as not to make my brother or sister fall into sin.
The Question about Food Offered to Idols

1: Am I not a free man? Am I not an apostle? Haven't I seen Jesus our Lord? And aren't you the result of my work for the Lord?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
2: Even if others do not accept me as an apostle, surely you do! Because of your life in union with the Lord you yourselves are proof of the fact that I am an apostle.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
3: When people criticize me, this is how I defend myself:
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
4: Haven't I the right to be given food and drink for my work?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
5: Haven't I the right to follow the example of the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter, by taking a Christian wife with me on my travels?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
6: Or are Barnabas and I the only ones who have to work for our living?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
7: What soldier ever has to pay his own expenses in the army? What farmer does not eat the grapes from his own vineyard? What shepherd does not use the milk from his own sheep?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
8: I don't have to limit myself to these everyday examples, because the Law says the same thing.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
9: We read in the Law of Moses, “Do not muzzle an ox when you are using it to thresh corn.” Now, is God concerned about oxen?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
Deut 25.4; 1 Tim 5.18
10: Didn't he really mean us when he said that? Of course that was written for us. The one who ploughs and the one who reaps should do their work in the hope of getting a share of the crop.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
11: We have sown spiritual seed among you. Is it too much if we reap material benefits from you?
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
12:
If others have the right to expect this from you, haven't we an even greater right?
But we haven't made use of this right. Instead, we have endured everything in order not to put any obstacle in the way of the Good News about Christ.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
13: Surely you know that the men who work in the Temple get their food from the Temple and that those who offer the sacrifices on the altar get a share of the sacrifices.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
Deut 18.1
14: In the same way, the Lord has ordered that those who preach the gospel should get their living from it.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
15: But I haven't made use of any of these rights, nor am I writing this now in order to claim such rights for myself. I would rather die first! Nobody is going to turn my rightful boast into empty words!
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
16: I have no right to boast just because I preach the gospel. After all, I am under orders to do so. And how terrible it would be for me if I did not preach the gospel!
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
17: If I did my work as a matter of free choice, then I could expect to be paid; but I do it as a matter of duty, because God has entrusted me with this task.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
18: What pay do I get, then? It is the privilege of preaching the Good News without charging for it, without claiming my rights in my work for the gospel.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
19: I am a free man, nobody's slave; but I make myself everybody's slave in order to win as many people as possible.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
20: While working with the Jews, I live like a Jew in order to win them; and even though I myself am not subject to the Law of Moses, I live as though I were when working with those who are, in order to win them.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
21: In the same way, when working with Gentiles, I live like a Gentile, outside the Jewish Law, in order to win Gentiles. This does not mean that I don't obey God's law; I am really under Christ's law.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
22: Among the weak in faith I become weak like one of them, in order to win them. So I become all things to all people, that I may save some of them by whatever means are possible.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
23: All this I do for the gospel's sake, in order to share in its blessings.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
24: Surely you know that many runners take part in a race, but only one of them wins the prize. Run, then, in such a way as to win the prize.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
25: Every athlete in training submits to strict discipline, in order to be crowned with a wreath that will not last; but we do it for one that will last for ever.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
26: That is why I run straight for the finishing line; that is why I am like a boxer who does not waste his punches.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle
27: I harden my body with blows and bring it under complete control, to keep myself from being disqualified after having called others to the contest.
Rights and Duties of an Apostle

1: I want you to remember, my brothers and sisters, what happened to our ancestors who followed Moses. They were all under the protection of the cloud, and all passed safely through the Red Sea.
Warnings against Idols
2: In the cloud and in the sea they were all baptized as followers of Moses.
Warnings against Idols
3: All ate the same spiritual bread
Warnings against Idols
4: and drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from the spiritual rock that went with them; and that rock was Christ himself.
Warnings against Idols
5: But even then God was not pleased with most of them, and so their dead bodies were scattered over the desert.
Warnings against Idols
6: Now, all this is an example for us, to warn us not to desire evil things, as they did,
Warnings against Idols
Num 11.4
7: nor to worship idols, as some of them did. As the scripture says, “The people sat down to a feast which turned into an orgy of drinking and sex.”
Warnings against Idols
8: We must not be guilty of sexual immorality, as some of them were — and in one day 23,000 of them fell dead.
Warnings against Idols
9:
We must not put the Lord
 to the test, as some of them did — and they were killed by snakes.
Warnings against Idols
10.9 the Lord; some manuscripts have Christ.; Num 21.5–6
10: We must not complain, as some of them did — and they were destroyed by the Angel of Death.
Warnings against Idols
11: All these things happened to them as examples for others, and they were written down as a warning for us. For we live at a time when the end is about to come.
Warnings against Idols
12: Those who think they are standing firm had better be careful that they do not fall.
Warnings against Idols
13: Every test that you have experienced is the kind that normally comes to people. But God keeps his promise, and he will not allow you to be tested beyond your power to remain firm; at the time you are put to the test, he will give you the strength to endure it, and so provide you with a way out.
Warnings against Idols
14: So then, my dear friends, keep away from the worship of idols.
Warnings against Idols
15: I speak to you as sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
Warnings against Idols
16: The cup we use in the Lord's Supper and for which we give thanks to God: when we drink from it, we are sharing in the blood of Christ. And the bread we break: when we eat it, we are sharing in the body of Christ.
Warnings against Idols
17: Because there is the one loaf of bread, all of us, though many, are one body, for we all share the same loaf.
Warnings against Idols
18: Consider the people of Israel; those who eat what is offered in sacrifice share in the altar's service to God.
Warnings against Idols
Lev 7.6
19: Do I imply, then, that an idol or the food offered to it really amounts to anything?
Warnings against Idols
20: No! What I am saying is that what is sacrificed on pagan altars is offered to demons, not to God. And I do not want you to be partners with demons.
Warnings against Idols
Deut 32.17 (LXX)
21: You cannot drink from the Lord's cup and also from the cup of demons; you cannot eat at the Lord's table and also at the table of demons.
Warnings against Idols
22: Or do we want to make the Lord jealous? Do we think that we are stronger than he?
Warnings against Idols
Deut 32.21
23: “We are allowed to do anything,” so they say. That is true, but not everything is good. “We are allowed to do anything” — but not everything is helpful.
Warnings against Idols
24: None of you should be looking to your own interests, but to the interests of others.
25: You are free to eat anything sold in the meat market, without asking any questions because of your conscience.
26: For, as the scripture says, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
27: If an unbeliever invites you to a meal and you decide to go, eat what is set before you, without asking any questions because of your conscience.
28: But if someone says to you, “This food was offered to idols,” then do not eat that food, for the sake of the one who told you and for conscience' sake —
29:
that is, not your own conscience, but the other person's conscience.
“Well, then,” someone asks, “why should my freedom to act be limited by another person's conscience?
30: If I thank God for my food, why should anyone criticize me about food for which I give thanks?”
31: Well, whatever you do, whether you eat or drink, do it all for God's glory.
32: Live in such a way as to cause no trouble either to Jews or Gentiles or to the church of God.
33: Just do as I do; I try to please everyone in all that I do, not thinking of my own good, but of the good of all, so that they might be saved.

1: Imitate me, then, just as I imitate Christ.
Covering the Head in Worship
1 Cor 4.16; Phil 3.17
2: I praise you because you always remember me and follow the teachings that I have handed on to you.
Covering the Head in Worship
3: But I want you to understand that Christ is supreme over every man, the husband is supreme over his wife, and God is supreme over Christ.
Covering the Head in Worship
4: So a man who prays or proclaims God's message in public worship with his head covered disgraces Christ.
Covering the Head in Worship
5: And any woman who prays or proclaims God's message in public worship with nothing on her head disgraces her husband; there is no difference between her and a woman whose head has been shaved.
Covering the Head in Worship
6: If the woman does not cover her head, she might as well cut her hair. And since it is a shameful thing for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair, she should cover her head.
Covering the Head in Worship
7: A man has no need to cover his head, because he reflects the image and glory of God. But woman reflects the glory of man;
Covering the Head in Worship
8: for man was not created from woman, but woman from man.
Covering the Head in Worship
9: Nor was man created for woman's sake, but woman was created for man's sake.
Covering the Head in Worship
10: On account of the angels, then, a woman should have a covering over her head to show that she is under her husband's authority.
Covering the Head in Worship
11: In our life in the Lord, however, woman is not independent of man, nor is man independent of woman.
Covering the Head in Worship
12: For as woman was made from man, in the same way man is born of woman; and it is God who brings everything into existence.
Covering the Head in Worship
13: Judge for yourselves whether it is proper for a woman to pray to God in public worship with nothing on her head.
Covering the Head in Worship
14: Why, nature itself teaches you that long hair on a man is a disgrace,
Covering the Head in Worship
15: but on a woman it is a thing of beauty. Her long hair has been given her to serve as a covering.
Covering the Head in Worship
16: But if anyone wants to argue about it, all I have to say is that neither we nor the churches of God have any other custom in worship.
The Lord's Supper
17: In the following instructions, however, I do not praise you, because your meetings for worship actually do more harm than good.
The Lord's Supper
18: In the first place, I have been told that there are opposing groups in your meetings; and this I believe is partly true.
The Lord's Supper
19: (No doubt there must be divisions among you so that the ones who are in the right may be clearly seen.)
The Lord's Supper
20: When you meet together as a group, it is not the Lord's Supper that you eat.
The Lord's Supper
21: For as you eat, you each go ahead with your own meal, so that some are hungry while others get drunk.
The Lord's Supper
22: Haven't you got your own homes in which to eat and drink? Or would you rather despise the church of God and put to shame the people who are in need? What do you expect me to say to you about this? Shall I praise you? Of course I don't!
The Lord's Supper
23: For I received from the Lord the teaching that I passed on to you: that the Lord Jesus, on the night he was betrayed, took a piece of bread,
The Lord's Supper
24: gave thanks to God, broke it, and said, “This is my body, which is for you. Do this in memory of me.”
25: In the same way, after the supper he took the cup and said, “This cup is God's new covenant, sealed with my blood. Whenever you drink it, do so in memory of me.”
26: This means that every time you eat this bread and drink from this cup you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
27: It follows that if anyone eats the Lord's bread or drinks from his cup in a way that dishonours him, he or she is guilty of sin against the Lord's body and blood.
28: So then, you should all examine yourselves first, and then eat the bread and drink from the cup.
29: For if people do not recognize the meaning of the Lord's body when they eat the bread and drink from the cup, they bring judgement on themselves as they eat and drink.
30: That is why many of you are weak and ill, and several have died.
31: If we would examine ourselves first, we would not come under God's judgement.
32: But we are judged and punished by the Lord, so that we shall not be condemned together with the world.
33: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you gather together to eat the Lord's Supper, wait for one another.
34: Anyone who is hungry should eat at home, so that you will not come under God's judgement as you meet together. As for the other matters, I will settle them when I come.

1:
Now, concerning what you wrote about the gifts from the Holy Spirit.
I want you to know the truth about them, my brothers and sisters.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
2: You know that while you were still heathen, you were led astray in many ways to the worship of lifeless idols.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
3: I want you to know that no one who is led by God's Spirit can say “A curse on Jesus!” and no one can confess “Jesus is Lord”, without being guided by the Holy Spirit.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
4: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but the same Spirit gives them.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
5: There are different ways of serving, but the same Lord is served.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
6: There are different abilities to perform service, but the same God gives ability to all for their particular service.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
7: The Spirit's presence is shown in some way in each person for the good of all.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
8: The Spirit gives one person a message full of wisdom, while to another person the same Spirit gives a message full of knowledge.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
9: One and the same Spirit gives faith to one person, while to another person he gives the power to heal.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
10: The Spirit gives one person the power to work miracles; to another, the gift of speaking God's message; and to yet another, the ability to tell the difference between gifts that come from the Spirit and those that do not. To one person he gives the ability to speak in strange tongues, and to another he gives the ability to explain what is said.
Gifts from the Holy Spirit
11: But it is one and the same Spirit who does all this; as he wishes, he gives a different gift to each person.
One Body with Many Parts
12: Christ is like a single body, which has many parts; it is still one body, even though it is made up of different parts.
One Body with Many Parts
13: In the same way, all of us, whether Jews or Gentiles, whether slaves or free, have been baptized into the one body by the same Spirit, and we have all been given the one Spirit to drink.
One Body with Many Parts
14: For the body itself is not made up of only one part, but of many parts.
One Body with Many Parts
15: If the foot were to say, “Because I am not a hand, I don't belong to the body,” that would not keep it from being a part of the body.
One Body with Many Parts
16: And if the ear were to say, “Because I am not an eye, I don't belong to the body,” that would not keep it from being a part of the body.
One Body with Many Parts
17: If the whole body were just an eye, how could it hear? And if it were only an ear, how could it smell?
One Body with Many Parts
18: As it is, however, God put every different part in the body just as he wanted it to be.
One Body with Many Parts
19: There would not be a body if it were all only one part!
One Body with Many Parts
20: As it is, there are many parts but one body.
One Body with Many Parts
21: So then, the eye cannot say to the hand, “I don't need you!” Nor can the head say to the feet, “Well, I don't need you!”
One Body with Many Parts
22: On the contrary, we cannot do without the parts of the body that seem to be weaker;
One Body with Many Parts
23: and those parts that we think aren't worth very much are the ones which we treat with greater care; while the parts of the body which don't look very nice are treated with special modesty,
One Body with Many Parts
24: which the more beautiful parts do not need. God himself has put the body together in such a way as to give greater honour to those parts that need it.
25: And so there is no division in the body, but all its different parts have the same concern for one another.
26: If one part of the body suffers, all the other parts suffer with it; if one part is praised, all the other parts share its happiness.
27: All of you are Christ's body, and each one is a part of it.
28: In the church God has put all in place: in the first place apostles, in the second place prophets, and in the third place teachers; then those who perform miracles, followed by those who are given the power to heal or to help others or to direct them or to speak in strange tongues.
29: They are not all apostles or prophets or teachers. Not everyone has the power to work miracles
30: or to heal diseases or to speak in strange tongues or to explain what is said.
31:
Set your hearts, then, on the more important gifts.
Best of all, however, is the following way.

1: I may be able to speak the languages of human beings and even of angels, but if I have no love, my speech is no more than a noisy gong or a clanging bell.
Love
2: I may have the gift of inspired preaching; I may have all knowledge and understand all secrets; I may have all the faith needed to move mountains — but if I have no love, I am nothing.
Love
3:
I may give away everything I have, and even give up my body to be burnt
 — but if I have no love, this does me no good.
Love
13.3 to be burnt; some manuscripts have in order to boast.
4: Love is patient and kind; it is not jealous or conceited or proud;
Love
5: love is not ill-mannered or selfish or irritable; love does not keep a record of wrongs;
Love
6: love is not happy with evil, but is happy with the truth.
Love
7: Love never gives up; and its faith, hope, and patience never fail.
Love
8: Love is eternal. There are inspired messages, but they are temporary; there are gifts of speaking in strange tongues, but they will cease; there is knowledge, but it will pass.
Love
9: For our gifts of knowledge and of inspired messages are only partial;
Love
10: but when what is perfect comes, then what is partial will disappear.
Love
11: When I was a child, my speech, feelings, and thinking were all those of a child; now that I have grown up, I have no more use for childish ways.
Love
12: What we see now is like a dim image in a mirror; then we shall see face to face. What I know now is only partial; then it will be complete — as complete as God's knowledge of me.
Love
13: Meanwhile these three remain: faith, hope, and love; and the greatest of these is love.
Love

1: It is love, then, that you should strive for. Set your hearts on spiritual gifts, especially the gift of proclaiming God's message.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
2: Those who speak in strange tongues do not speak to others but to God, because no one understands them. They are speaking secret truths by the power of the Spirit.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
3: But those who proclaim God's message speak to people and give them help, encouragement, and comfort.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
4: Those who speak in strange tongues help only themselves, but those who proclaim God's message help the whole church.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
5: I would like all of you to speak in strange tongues; but I would rather that you had the gift of proclaiming God's message. For the person who proclaims God's message is of greater value than the one who speaks in strange tongues — unless there is someone present who can explain what is said, so that the whole church may be helped.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
6: So when I come to you, my brothers and sisters, what use will I be to you if I speak in strange tongues? Not a bit, unless I bring you some revelation from God or some knowledge or some inspired message, or some teaching.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
7: Take such lifeless musical instruments as the flute or the harp — how will anyone know the tune that is being played unless the notes are sounded distinctly?
More about Gifts from the Spirit
8: And if the one who plays the bugle does not sound a clear call, who will prepare for battle?
More about Gifts from the Spirit
9: In the same way, how will anyone understand what you are talking about if your message given in strange tongues is not clear? Your words will vanish in the air!
More about Gifts from the Spirit
10: There are many different languages in the world, yet none of them is without meaning.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
11: But if I do not know the language being spoken, those who use it will be foreigners to me and I will be a foreigner to them.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
12: Since you are eager to have the gifts of the Spirit, you must try above everything else to make greater use of those which help to build up the church.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
13: The person who speaks in strange tongues, then, must pray for the gift to explain what is said.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
14: For if I pray in this way, my spirit prays indeed, but my mind has no part in it.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
15: What should I do, then? I will pray with my spirit, but I will pray also with my mind; I will sing with my spirit, but I will sing also with my mind.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
16: When you give thanks to God in spirit only, how can ordinary people taking part in the meeting say “Amen” to your prayer of thanksgiving? They have no way of knowing what you are saying.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
17: Even if your prayer of thanks to God is quite good, other people are not helped at all.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
18: I thank God that I speak in strange tongues much more than any of you.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
19: But in church worship I would rather speak five words that can be understood, in order to teach others, than speak thousands of words in strange tongues.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
20: Do not be like children in your thinking, my brothers and sisters; be children so far as evil is concerned, but be grown-up in your thinking.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
21:
In the Scriptures it is written:
“By means of people speaking strange languages
I will speak to my people, says the Lord.
I will speak through lips of foreigners,
but even then my people will not listen to me.”
More about Gifts from the Spirit
22: So then, the gift of speaking in strange tongues is proof for unbelievers, not for believers, while the gift of proclaiming God's message is proof for believers, not for unbelievers.
More about Gifts from the Spirit
23: If, then, the whole church meets together and everyone starts speaking in strange tongues — and if some ordinary people or unbelievers come in, won't they say that you are all crazy?
More about Gifts from the Spirit
24: But if everyone is proclaiming God's message when some unbelievers or ordinary people come in, they will be convinced of their sin by what they hear. They will be judged by all they hear,
More about Gifts from the Spirit
25: their secret thoughts will be brought into the open, and they will bow down and worship God, confessing, “Truly God is here among you!”
26: This is what I mean, my brothers and sisters. When you meet for worship, one person has a hymn, another a teaching, another a revelation from God, another a message in strange tongues, and still another the explanation of what is said. Everything must be of help to the church.
27: If someone is going to speak in strange tongues, two or three at the most should speak, one after the other, and someone else must explain what is being said.
28: But if no one is there who can explain, then the one who speaks in strange tongues must be quiet and speak only to himself and to God.
29: Two or three who are given God's message should speak, while the others are to judge what they say.
30: But if someone sitting in the meeting receives a message from God, the one who is speaking should stop.
31: All of you may proclaim God's message, one by one, so that everyone will learn and be encouraged.
32: The gift of proclaiming God's message should be under the speaker's control,
33:
because God does not want us to be in disorder but in harmony and peace.
As in all the churches of God's people,
34: the women should keep quiet in the meetings. They are not allowed to speak; as the Jewish Law says, they must not be in charge.
35: If they want to find out about something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is a disgraceful thing for a woman to speak in church.
36: Or could it be that the word of God came from you? Or are you the only ones to whom it came?
37: If anyone supposes he is God's messenger or has a spiritual gift, he must realize that what I am writing to you is the Lord's command.
38: But if he does not pay attention to this, pay no attention to him.
39: So then, my brothers and sisters, set your hearts on proclaiming God's message, but do not forbid the speaking in strange tongues.
40: Everything must be done in a proper and orderly way.

1: And now I want to remind you, my brothers and sisters, of the Good News which I preached to you, which you received, and on which your faith stands firm.
The Resurrection of Christ
2: That is the gospel, the message that I preached to you. You are saved by the gospel if you hold firmly to it — unless it was for nothing that you believed.
The Resurrection of Christ
3: I passed on to you what I received, which is of the greatest importance: that Christ died for our sins, as written in the Scriptures;
The Resurrection of Christ
4: that he was buried and that he was raised to life three days later, as written in the Scriptures;
The Resurrection of Christ
5: that he appeared to Peter and then to all twelve apostles.
The Resurrection of Christ
6: Then he appeared to more than 500 of his followers at once, most of whom are still alive, although some have died.
The Resurrection of Christ
7: Then he appeared to James, and afterwards to all the apostles.
The Resurrection of Christ
8: Last of all he appeared also to me — even though I am like someone whose birth was abnormal.
The Resurrection of Christ
Acts 9.3–6; 15.8 whose birth was abnormal; or who was born at the wrong time.
9: For I am the least of all the apostles — I do not even deserve to be called an apostle, because I persecuted God's church.
The Resurrection of Christ
Acts 8.3
10: But by God's grace I am what I am, and the grace that he gave me was not without effect. On the contrary, I have worked harder than any of the other apostles, although it was not really my own doing, but God's grace working with me.
The Resurrection of Christ
11: So then, whether it came from me or from them, this is what we all preach, and this is what you believe.
Our Resurrection
12: Now, since our message is that Christ has been raised from death, how can some of you say that the dead will not be raised to life?
Our Resurrection
13: If that is true, it means that Christ was not raised;
Our Resurrection
14: and if Christ has not been raised from death, then we have nothing to preach and you have nothing to believe.
Our Resurrection
15: More than that, we are shown to be lying about God, because we said that he raised Christ from death — but if it is true that the dead are not raised to life, then he did not raise Christ.
Our Resurrection
16: For if the dead are not raised, neither has Christ been raised.
Our Resurrection
17: And if Christ has not been raised, then your faith is a delusion and you are still lost in your sins.
Our Resurrection
18: It would also mean that the believers in Christ who have died are lost.
Our Resurrection
19:
If our hope in Christ is good for this life only and no more,
 then we deserve more pity than anyone else in all the world.
Our Resurrection
15.19 If our hope in Christ is good for this life only and no more; or If all we have in this life is our hope in Christ.
20: But the truth is that Christ has been raised from death, as the guarantee that those who sleep in death will also be raised.
Our Resurrection
21: For just as death came by means of a man, in the same way the rising from death comes by means of a man.
Our Resurrection
22: For just as all people die because of their union with Adam, in the same way all will be raised to life because of their union with Christ.
Our Resurrection
23: But each one will be raised in the right order: Christ, first of all; then, at the time of his coming, those who belong to him.
Our Resurrection
24: Then the end will come; Christ will overcome all spiritual rulers, authorities, and powers, and will hand over the Kingdom to God the Father.
25: For Christ must rule until God defeats all enemies and puts them under his feet.
26: The last enemy to be defeated will be death.
27:
For the scripture says, “God put 
all
 things under his feet.” It is clear, of course, that the words “all things” do not include God himself, who puts all things under Christ.
28: But when all things have been placed under Christ's rule, then he himself, the Son, will place himself under God, who placed all things under him; and God will rule completely over all.
29: Now, what about those people who are baptized for the dead? What do they hope to accomplish? If it is true, as some claim, that the dead are not raised to life, why are those people being baptized for the dead?
30: And as for us — why would we run the risk of danger every hour?
31: My brothers and sisters, I face death every day! The pride I have in you, in our life in union with Christ Jesus our Lord, makes me declare this.
32: If I have, as it were, fought “wild beasts” here in Ephesus simply from human motives, what have I gained? But if the dead are not raised to life, then, as the saying goes, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we will die.”
33: Do not be fooled. “Bad companions ruin good character.”
34: Come back to your right senses and stop your sinful ways. I declare to your shame that some of you do not know God.
35: Someone will ask, “How can the dead be raised to life? What kind of body will they have?”
36: You fool! When you sow a seed in the ground, it does not sprout to life unless it dies.
37: And what you sow is a bare seed, perhaps a grain of wheat or some other grain, not the full-bodied plant that will later grow up.
38: God provides that seed with the body he wishes; he gives each seed its own proper body.
39: And the flesh of living beings is not all the same kind of flesh; human beings have one kind of flesh, animals another, birds another, and fish another.
40: And there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies; the beauty that belongs to heavenly bodies is different from the beauty that belongs to earthly bodies.
41: The sun has its own beauty, the moon another beauty, and the stars a different beauty; and even among stars there are different kinds of beauty.
42: This is how it will be when the dead are raised to life. When the body is buried, it is mortal; when raised, it will be immortal.
43: When buried, it is ugly and weak; when raised, it will be beautiful and strong.
44: When buried, it is a physical body; when raised, it will be a spiritual body. There is, of course, a physical body, so there has to be a spiritual body.
45: For the scripture says, “The first man, Adam, was created a living being”; but the last Adam is the life-giving Spirit.
46: It is not the spiritual that comes first, but the physical, and then the spiritual.
47: The first Adam, made of earth, came from the earth; the second Adam came from heaven.
48: Those who belong to the earth are like the one who was made of earth; those who are of heaven are like the one who came from heaven.
49:
Just as we wear the likeness of the man made of earth, so we will wear
 the likeness of the Man from heaven.
15.49 we will wear; some manuscripts have let us wear.
50: What I mean, brothers and sisters, is that what is made of flesh and blood cannot share in God's Kingdom, and what is mortal cannot possess immortality.
51-52: Listen to this secret truth: we shall not all die, but when the last trumpet sounds, we shall all be changed in an instant, as quickly as the blinking of an eye. For when the trumpet sounds, the dead will be raised, never to die again, and we shall all be changed.
53: For what is mortal must be changed into what is immortal; what will die must be changed into what cannot die.
54: So when this takes place, and the mortal has been changed into the immortal, then the scripture will come true: “Death is destroyed; victory is complete!”
55:
“Where, Death, is your victory?
Where, Death, is your power to hurt?”
56: Death gets its power to hurt from sin, and sin gets its power from the Law.
57: But thanks be to God who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!
58: So then, my dear brothers and sisters, stand firm and steady. Keep busy always in your work for the Lord, since you know that nothing you do in the Lord's service is ever useless.

1: Now, concerning what you wrote about the money to be raised to help God's people in Judea. You must do what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
The Offering for Fellow-believers
2: Every Sunday each of you must put aside some money, in proportion to what you have earned, and save it up, so that there will be no need to collect money when I come.
The Offering for Fellow-believers
3: After I come, I shall give letters of introduction to those you have approved, and send them to take your gift to Jerusalem.
The Offering for Fellow-believers
4: If it seems worthwhile for me to go, then they can go along with me.
Paul's Plans
5: I shall come to you after I have gone through Macedonia — for I have to go through Macedonia.
Paul's Plans
Acts 19.21
6: I shall probably spend some time with you, perhaps the whole winter, and then you can help me to continue my journey, wherever it is I shall go next.
Paul's Plans
7: I want to see you more than just briefly in passing; I hope to spend quite a long time with you, if the Lord allows.
Paul's Plans
8: I will stay here in Ephesus until the day of Pentecost.
Paul's Plans
9: There is a real opportunity here for great and worthwhile work, even though there are many opponents.
Paul's Plans
10: If Timothy comes your way, be sure to make him feel welcome among you, because he is working for the Lord, just as I am.
Paul's Plans
11: No one should look down on him, but you must help him to continue his trip in peace, so that he will come back to me; for I am expecting him back with the brothers and sisters.
Paul's Plans
12:
Now, about brother Apollos. I have often encouraged him to visit you with the other believers, but he is not completely convinced
 that he should go at this time. When he gets the chance, however, he will go.
Final Words
16.12 he is not completely convinced; or it is not at all God's will.
13: Be alert, stand firm in the faith, be brave, be strong.
Final Words
14: Do all your work in love.
Final Words
15: You know about Stephanas and his family; they are the first Christian converts in Achaia and have given themselves to the service of God's people. I beg you, my brothers and sisters,
Final Words
16: to follow the leadership of such people as these, and of anyone else who works and serves with them.
Final Words
17: I am happy about the coming of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus; they have made up for your absence
Final Words
18: and have cheered me up, just as they cheered you up. Such men as these deserve notice.
Final Words
19: The churches in the province of Asia send you their greetings; Aquila and Priscilla and the church that meets in their house send warm Christian greetings.
Final Words
Acts 18.2
20:
All the believers here send greetings.
Greet one another with a holy kiss.
Final Words
21:
With my own hand I write this: 
Greetings from Paul.
Final Words
22:
Whoever does not love the Lord — a curse on him!
Marana tha
 — Our Lord, come!
Final Words
23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
Final Words
24: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus.
